title: 3 Establishing Policies for PaymentStreamâ¢ Direct
body_text:
3	Establishing Policies for PaymentStreamâ¢ Direct
3.1	Policy on Internal Controls for PaymentStream Direct
You are responsible for
PaymentStream
Direct security practices and internal controls. Some policies may include but are not limited to the following:
Limiting access to
PaymentStream
Direct and its functionality to employees who need it to complete their job requirements
Ensuring that
PaymentStream
Direct users receive their one-time CPS Admin/Client Centre passwords in a secure and confidential manner (applies to individuals who do not already have a Client Centre username or whose Client Centre password is reset)
Ensuring that users have their own unique user IDs and donât share user IDs
Prohibiting the use of multiple user IDs for a single employee
Ensuring that
PaymentStream
Direct users are reviewed on a regular basis and that required action is taken to update or delete user profiles
Identifying and deleting
PaymentStream
Direct users who
â
are inactive for one year or more,
â
no longer require access to
PaymentStream
Direct, or
â
are no longer employees of your financial institution.
3.2	Policy on Privacy Requirements
Federal and provincial legislation influences and regulates
PaymentStream
Direct transactions (e.g., privacy legislation; human rights legislation). To address this issue, your financial institution must apply additional policies and procedures. If necessary, contact your corporate solicitor for assistance in establishing policies for these requirements.
END
